urn:contentItem:6JJH-00G3-RTSH-C3YF-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:01ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ M A MORTENSON WINS $610M BIOSECURITY CONSTRUCTION PROJECT TARGETING NEW WORLD SCREWWORM

M A Mortenson Company, a Minneapolis-based construction firm, has been awarded a $610 million contract by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to build the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas. The contract is part of a broader effort to curb the spread of New World screwworms in the United States. On March 9, 2026, officials warned of the threat if the screwworm migrates north from Mexico, with the USDA signaling urgency as cases are reported in Tamaulipas near the Texas border. As of March 4, 2026, there were 989 active cases nationwide, including 19 in Tamaulipas, up from 11 weeks earlier, illustrating the escalating risk to livestock. Officials said the project will help maintain a steady supply of sterilized flies used to disrupt the screwworm life cycle, a key component of border protection and the broader biosecurity strategy, with Mortenson coordinating construction alongside USDA efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ ANDREAS VLAHAKIS APPOINTED TO LEAD CAPITAL MARKETS AT GILBANE DEVELOPMENT

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development has named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, to operate from the firm's New York City office. He brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets across asset classes, with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.In this role, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane's funding strategy, report to James Patchett, president and CEO, and help shape the capital strategy, drive investment decisions, and nurture relationships with funding and equity partners. He will also lead transaction structuring and negotiations to position Gilbane to capitalize on evolving market opportunities.Previously, Vlahakis served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, leading equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities underscores his broad financial leadership as the firm pursues growth.Gilbane Development says the appointment aligns with its commitment to quality and innovation and positions the firm for growth by strengthening its capital discipline and investment program.Investors will likely be watching how Vlahakis's background translates into stronger capital relationships and execution in Gilbane's future real estate deals.

+ WKU FORMS GILBANE ALLIANCE TO DEVELOP NEW RESIDENCE HALL AND UPGRADES

The Western Kentucky University Student Government Association announced the formal approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane, designed to expand housing and campus facilities. The agreement was approved by WKU's Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, with Gilbane tasked to design and build a new residence hall to replace Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls. With the Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026, construction is authorized to commence. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is fully authorized and stated, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." Beyond housing, the SGA advanced several measures aimed at improving student resources. Resolution 7-26-S backs installing a crosswalk at a key intersection near housing and urges collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation to address student safety. Bill 17-26-S creates the Textbooks for Toppers program, which will provide $50 reimbursements for required course materials. Additional bills will supply essential academic items such as Scantrons and lab goggles to reduce out-of-pocket costs for students. Taken together, the developments reflect WKU's broader strategy to enhance the student experience through strategic housing development and targeted support for student resources.

+ PHASE II LEASING OPENS FOR GILBANE’S $135M BARNABY & 7TH DEVELOPMENT IN DC

Gilbane Development has moved Phase II of the Barnaby & 7th project into leasing, adding 229 units in four new buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II is scheduled for completion by summer 2026 and provides a mix of studio to three-bedroom units designed for households earning 50 percent and 80 percent of AMI. Twenty percent of the apartments will be Permanent Supportive Housing units, managed by the District's Department of Homeless Services.Phase I, The Upland, was finished in Spring 2025 and contains 169 apartments, including 34 PSH units. Income limits for Phase I range from 30 to 50 percent of AMI.The third phase is planned to add roughly 100 more rental units in a single building, bringing the project total to about 498 units upon completion.During Phase II construction, a new pedestrian walkway was built to connect all six buildings within the larger development. The District’s Housing Finance Agency estimates Phase II cost at about $135 million, illustrating Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing and enhanced community connectivity in Washington, D.C.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJC-BS63-RX5X-H41Y-00000-00Our View: Picks: Haaland, Hull for gov., Luján for U.S. Senate2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisBPg. 2Our View: Picks: Haaland, Hull for gov., Luján for U.S. SenateThe New Mexican

Governor: Democratic primaryThe Democratic primary race between Bernalillo County District Attorney Sam Bregman and former U.S. Rep. and Department of the Interior Secretary Deb Haaland features tough talk vs. soft speech.

It goes without saying that Haaland is a historic candidate - if elected, she could become the first Native female governor in the United States. But that's not why Democrats should choose her as their nominee.

The Laguna Pueblo tribal member would bring to the office her experience as interior secretary, where she supervised more than 70,000 employees and a budget of some $18 billion annually. She was responsible for protecting an estimated 1 million acres of land and water, advancing renewable energy projects and implementing the Federal Indian Boarding School initiative so the country can face its past.

Haaland has superior executive experience, crucial for a governor in charge of the executive branch. For doubters, there's one direct comparison. Haaland and Bregman both served as chairs of the state Democratic Party. He left the party in debt. She did not.

The two candidates have different views of how to move New Mexico beyond dependence on the oil and gas industry. In fact, Bregman held a fundraiser in 2025 at the Petroleum Club of Midland. For a Maryland-born, cowboy hat-wearing lawman, the affinity with Texas makes sense. However, this is about the best choice for governor of New Mexico. Haaland has taken stronger positions against reuse of produced water, in favor of renewable energy and for protection of Chaco Canyon from drilling.

We like that Haaland wants to address healthcare, providing coverage for more people in a state small enough to experiment with universal care. She is outspoken about the need to keep private equity out of utility management.

Beyond policy differences, Haaland is a collaborative leader, one who will listen to her Cabinet and other state employees. Most of all, she has spent her year of campaigning crisscrossing the state, meeting one-on-one and in small groups with tens of thousands of New Mexicans. She listens more than she speaks, giving her the on-the-ground knowledge she will need to tackle New Mexico problems. In the Democratic primary, The New Mexican endorses Deb Haaland.

Governor: Republican primary

This primary is wide open, with the sitting Rio Rancho mayor facing two business leaders with 40% of voters undecided. Anyone can win.

We have enjoyed talking to all the candidates who, at least in face-to-face conversations, avoid culture war issues and talk about specific policy solutions they have for New Mexico.

Duke Rodriguez, the cannabis CEO who doesn't believe Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham's childcare initiative is legal, is open to innovations that would place New Mexico in a better competitive situation. Businessman Doug Turner brings a thoughtful, reasoned approach to politics. Both Rodriguez and Turner came out of the Gary Johnson administration - whether working inside it as Rodriguez did, or directing a campaign as Turner did.

Quieter, but more experienced in actual governing is Gregg Hull, 12-year mayor of Rio Rancho, a successful, welcoming city. For 12 of the years that the city has been growing and thriving, Hull was the mayor.

He can spend 30 minutes discussing the ins and outs of the gross receipts tax system and how he would reform it. Hull believes government needs to streamline itself to deliver basic services - whether for kids in schools, tax relief for business owners or building infrastructure improvements efficiently.

We can't help but like that in an earlier incarnation, Hull worked delivering The Santa Fe New Mexican, even completing his morning paper route before taking his wife to the hospital where she delivered their first child. His vision is of a New Mexico where adult children return home to raise their own children, bringing joy to grandparents across the state. In the GOP primary, The New Mexican endorses Gregg Hull.

In addition to the Republican and Democratic nominees, independent Ken Miyagishima will be on the November ballot as a candidate for governor.

U.S. Senate

Sen. Ben Ray Luján will be reelected, a result we soundly endorse. But we also want to thank Air Force veteran and democratic socialist Matt Dodson, who worked overtime to gather the signatures necessary to be on the ballot. Dodson represents the left wing of the Democratic Party, pushing more mainstream Democrats to stop taking corporate PAC dollars, lessen their support for Israel and focus on putting people before big donors. His participation makes the primary a contest - and Luján, as well as voters, should be grateful.

Having an opponent offers a platform for Luján to present his solid record of accomplishment, from expanding federal compensation for survivors of radiation exposure, to introducing the Epstein Transparency Act seeking justice and accountability, to supporting protection of public lands. Despite partisan differences, Luján successfully lobbied to raise the cap on the Navajo-Gallup Water project and to cap the costs for the city of Gallup - winning $120 million to fund the project and a $76 million cap for Gallup. He is working to protect public lands from invasive mining, quickly responding to potential uranium mining that would harm the Rio Chama region.

This is a senator who participates in the big fights while never forgetting the needs of his state. In the 2026 Democratic primary, The New Mexican endorses Ben Ray Luján.

GOP write-in candidate Larry E. Marker could make the general election ballot if he receives enough write-in votes, some 2,351 for this race.

Primary endorsements

Friday: County Commission, sheriff and state legislative races

Saturday: Lieutenant governor, secretary of state and state land commissioner

Today: Governor and U.S. Senate

urn:contentItem:6JJ8-2KF3-RTRW-N12D-00000-00Ellomay Capital Consummates the Sale of its Indirect Holdings in Dorad Energy Ltd.2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:01ZLexisNexisPRESS RELEASESEllomay Capital Consummates the Sale of its Indirect Holdings in Dorad Energy Ltd.

Final Purchase Price is NIS 559.8 Million

Tel-Aviv, Israel, May 10, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --  Ellomay Capital Ltd. (NYSE American; TASE: ELLO) (“Ellomay” or the “Company”), a renewable energy and power generator and developer of renewable energy and power projects in Europe, USA and Israel, today announced the consummation of the sale of its holdings (50%) in Ellomay Luzon Energy Infrastructures Ltd. (“Ellomay Luzon Energy”) to the Amos Luzon Development and Energy Group Ltd. (the “Luzon Group”).

Ellomay Luzon Energy’s main asset is its holding of 33.75% of Dorad Energy Ltd. (“Dorad”). The consideration was based on a Dorad valuation of NIS 4.4 billion, i.e. a valuation of NIS 742.5 million for the Company’s indirect holdings in Dorad, net of 50% of Ellomay Luzon Energy’s net debt (outstanding bank loans minus cash and cash equivalents) in an amount of approximately NIS 182.7 million, resulting in a purchase price of approximately NIS 559.8 million.

Of the consideration received, an amount of approximately NIS 166.2 million was deposited in a bank account pledged to the trustee for the Company’s Series E Secured Debentures as financial collateral for the removal of the pledges placed on the Ellomay Luzon Energy shares sold to the Luzon Group. This amount will be used for the early repayment of the Series E Secured Debentures, scheduled for May 24, 2026, in an amount of approximately NIS 170 million.

Ran Fridrich, CEO and Board member of Ellomay commented: “We are pleased to complete this transaction, which reflects the significant value created through our investment in Ellomay Luzon Energy. The proceeds from the sale will support the development of our growing portfolio of projects and provide enhanced financial flexibility as we execute on our strategic plans. This transaction also enables us to further sharpen our focus on our core business activities as we continue to pursue long-term shareholder value creation.”

About Ellomay Capital Ltd.

Ellomay is an Israeli based company whose shares are registered with the NYSE American and with the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange under the trading symbol “ELLO”. Since 2009, Ellomay focuses its business in the renewable energy and power sectors in Europe, USA and Israel.

To date, Ellomay has evaluated numerous opportunities and invested significant funds in the renewable, clean energy and natural resources industries in Israel, Italy, Spain, the Netherlands and Texas, USA, including:

Approximately 335.9 MW of operating solar power plants in Spain (including a 300 MW solar plant in owned by Talasol, which is 51% owned by the Company) and 51% of approximately 38 MW of operating solar power plants in Italy;

Groen Gas Goor B.V., Groen Gas Oude-Tonge B.V. and Groen Gas Gelderland B.V., project companies operating anaerobic digestion plants in the Netherlands, with a green gas production capacity of approximately 3 million, 3.8 million and 9.5 million Nm3 per year, respectively;

83.333% of Ellomay Pumped Storage (2014) Ltd., which is involved in a project to construct a 156 MW pumped storage hydro power plant in the Manara Cliff, Israel;

51% of solar projects in Italy with an aggregate capacity of 160 MW that are under construction;

Solar projects in Italy with an aggregate capacity of 210 MW that have reached “ready to build” status; and

Solar projects in the Dallas Metropolitan area, Texas, USA with an aggregate capacity of approximately 38 MW that are connected to the grid, 11 MW that are currently in the test run phase prior to commercial operation and 14 MW that are under construction.

For more information about Ellomay, visit http://www.ellomay.com.

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-R573-S366-M1N0-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE PROJECT IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation is making its entry into the Oklahoma market by securing a contract for the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement project. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry Group in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver this significant infrastructure upgrade. This partnership marks the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the Oklahoma DOT, highlighting the company's ambition to expand its regional presence.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, signifies a major investment in modernizing a critical transportation corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has been operational since 1945 and serves as an essential link for local communities, businesses, and visitors traveling to the Lake Texoma area. With the new bridge, Zachry Construction aims to enhance mobility, safety, and economic activity in southeastern Oklahoma.Zachry and Traylor Bros. will construct a new four-lane bridge that includes modern features such as bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also entails the demolition of the existing bridge, with traffic being maintained on it during the construction phase to minimize disruption to the traveling public. Echoing the significance of this venture, Travis Mross, president of Zachry Construction, stated, 'This project is an important milestone for Zachry, as it strengthens a vital connection for the communities around Lake Texoma.'The project is designed to improve safety, reliability, and resilience for the future. It aims to address the increasing traffic demands in the area while ensuring that local commerce and tourism continue to flourish. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has set a completion target of 1st October 2029 for the Roosevelt Bridge replacement project, which is expected to elevate the standard of infrastructure in the region.Zachry Construction Corporation, a third-generation family business with over a century of experience, will leverage its extensive expertise in heavy and highway construction for this initiative. The joint venture with Traylor Bros., known for its proficiency in complex bridge projects, promises to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and maintains mobility throughout construction. Together, they are poised to provide lasting value to the state and its communities.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORP. ELEVATES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AMID STRATEGIC GROWTH

Zachry Construction Corp. has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, marking a significant transition in leadership for the San Antonio-based company. Mross, who began his career with the family-owned business as an intern in 2000, officially took on the presidential role on 29th April 2026.Over the years, Mross has steadily advanced through the ranks, culminating in his previous position as executive vice president for the past six years. In this role, he played a crucial part in shaping Zachry Construction's strategic direction and enhancing operational efficiency. He succeeds David Zachry, who will now concentrate solely on his responsibilities as CEO."Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," David Zachry stated. He emphasized Mross's deep understanding of the business and his contributions to instilling discipline and accountability, which are essential for the company's continued success. Under Mross's leadership, Zachry Construction aims to maintain its momentum and explore new opportunities.Zachry Construction Corp. is recognized for its extensive work in heavy civil, roadway, and infrastructure projects, with over 6,000 completed initiatives in both public and private sectors. The company is currently expanding its operations in the water and wastewater infrastructure domain, considered one of the fastest-growing segments in the construction industry. Recent projects include water storage and dams for various authorities, and the acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. has further strengthened its capabilities in this area.As president, Mross will oversee operations across multiple sectors, including water, transportation, underground utilities, and civil sitework. His priorities will focus on strategic growth, operational excellence, and nurturing a robust corporate culture while maintaining a gritty mindset. Mross stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW LEASE NEAR LA VILLITA

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL AND NABTU JOIN FORCES TO ENHANCE NUCLEAR CONSTRUCTION TRAINING

Bechtel and North America's Building Trades Unions (NABTU) have announced a new partnership aimed at modernizing apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction. This collaboration comes as the U.S. seeks to bolster its nuclear infrastructure, particularly with the rise of large-scale reactors and Small Modular Reactors (SMRs). The Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) was signed on the 5th of May, 2026, marking a significant step in workforce development for the nuclear sector.The strategic agreement highlights the alignment between Bechtel, a leader in nuclear infrastructure construction, and NABTU, which is the largest federation of skilled construction unions in North America. The partnership aims to enhance apprenticeship pathways and expand specialized training that focuses on the technical requirements and quality standards necessary for modern nuclear projects. This initiative is especially critical as the industry prepares for increased demand in nuclear energy solutions.Under the terms of the MOU, Bechtel and NABTU will work collaboratively to identify the specialized craft skills needed for nuclear construction, ensuring that apprenticeship training is appropriately aligned. The organizations plan to modernize curricula at union training centers that support cutting-edge nuclear construction methods and technologies. Additionally, they will coordinate recruitment efforts through union networks to create more pathways into high-skill careers in building large reactors and SMRs.Brendan Bechtel, Chair and CEO of Bechtel, emphasized the importance of this partnership in delivering nuclear power plants that require exceptional craftsmanship and a commitment to safety. He noted that the collaboration builds upon their successful history with NABTU, including projects such as the Vogtle Units 3 and 4, which are the first new nuclear reactors constructed in the U.S. in over 30 years. Bechtel’s current initiatives, including the Natrium Demonstration Project, reflect a forward-looking approach toward nuclear innovation.NABTU President Sean McGarvey echoed this sentiment, stating that the construction of the next generation of nuclear projects demands high levels of skill and precision, starting with robust apprenticeship training. The agreement aims to ensure a steady pipeline of job-ready craft professionals, alleviating potential labor shortages for upcoming projects. As the U.S. forges ahead with nuclear energy advancements, this partnership between Bechtel and NABTU will play a crucial role in developing the skilled workforce necessary to meet future energy demands.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ANNOUNCED TO BOOST MINING PROJECTS IN CHILE

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverr a Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. This collaboration aims to effectively support the delivery of significant mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026.As investments in mining continue to surge in the region, the demand for efficient project execution that adheres to rigorous safety and quality standards has never been higher. The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies; the engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA’s established reputation for direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Lucy Martin, President of the Mining & Metals division of the global firm, emphasized the necessity for a refined execution model in delivering complex projects at scale. She stated, "This partnership reflects a deliberate approach to execution, combining our end-to-end delivery capability with EIMISA’s strong construction presence in Chile to strengthen how we deliver across the region."Dar o Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, echoed this sentiment, asserting that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies. He noted, "Bechtel’s global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile’s growing portfolio of complex projects."With a history of over 400 completed projects and a workforce exceeding 8,000, EIMISA has positioned itself as a vital partner in Latin America’s construction sector. The collaboration with the global engineering firm is poised to enhance the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects, contributing to the region's economic growth and development.

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO ENHANCE PIPEFITTING SKILLS IN LAKE CHARLES

Bechtel, a leading global engineering and construction firm, has announced a partnership with SOWELA Technical Community College to introduce a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA’s Main Campus in Lake Charles. This initiative aims to equip local students with the essential skills needed for a career in pipefitting, a critical trade in various industries including manufacturing and power generation. The program is set to commence on 26th May 2026 and will run until 13th November 2026.The training program is designed to provide students with a comprehensive introduction to the pipefitting trade, covering core job responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used in the field. Participants will engage in hands-on learning, focusing on the installation and repair of high- and low-pressure pipe systems, which are vital to the functioning of manufacturing facilities, power generation plants, and building heating and cooling systems.Classes will be held Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., allowing participants to gain a thorough understanding of the pipefitting profession over the course of the program. Upon successful completion, students will earn an OSHA 10-hour safety credential and will be well-prepared to pursue the National Center for Construction Education and Research (NCCER) Pipefitting certifications, enhancing their employability in a competitive job market.To qualify for employment consideration, successful program completers must pass a background check and drug screening, ensuring that they meet industry standards. Interested individuals can find more information or register for the program by visiting www.sowela.edu/pipefitting. For further inquiries, SOWELA’s Office of Workforce Solutions can be contacted at (337) 421-6560 or via email at workforce@sowela.edu.This collaboration between Bechtel and SOWELA highlights the commitment of both organizations to workforce development in the Lake Charles area, addressing the growing demand for skilled labor in the construction and manufacturing sectors. By providing accessible training opportunities, the program aims to empower local residents and contribute to the region 's economic growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INNSBROOK OFFICE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. The expansion is supported by local incentives from Henrico County, which aims to attract and retain businesses in the area. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The new office space will allow DPR Construction to bring together teams and resources, fostering collaboration and innovation within the company. This growth is expected to create additional job opportunities in the region, further contributing to Henrico's economic development goals. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. This aligns with the company's mission to lead in sustainable construction practices while also providing a conducive work environment for its employees. The expansion is anticipated to be completed within the next year, further solidifying DPR Construction's commitment to the region.In addition to the expansion, DPR Construction has been actively involved in various community initiatives, underscoring its dedication to corporate social responsibility. As the company prepares for this new chapter, stakeholders are keenly watching how this expansion will impact both the firm and the local economy.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION SECURES ROLE IN THE BORING COMPANY'S DUBAI LOOP INITIATIVE

CHANTILLY, Va. — Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) has been awarded a contract by The Boring Company (TBC) to provide professional services in support of the Dubai Loop project, an underground transportation initiative being developed in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. This contract, dated 5th May 2026, spans nine months and marks new work for Parsons.Under the terms of the agreement, Parsons will serve as TBC’s delegated program manager, aiding in design-build activities for the Dubai Loop project. The services provided will include Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase. Additionally, Parsons will carry out multidisciplinary reviews of designs across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility domains.Pierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, emphasized the company's longstanding commitment to infrastructure development in the UAE, stating, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." He added that the partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's vision to foster strategic partnerships and enhance advanced technologies as part of the Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan.The Dubai Loop project, in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), aims to expand the city’s transport ecosystem through a series of passenger transport tunnels and stations. Parsons has a history of working with the RTA, having contributed to significant transportation infrastructure projects in the Emirate since 2005, including the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge projects.Parsons Corporation has managed over 250 complex tunneling projects globally and has extensive experience in road and highway development, covering more than 10,000 miles across six continents. As a leading provider of disruptive technologies in national security and global infrastructure, Parsons is well-positioned to contribute to the innovative Dubai Loop initiative, enhancing urban mobility and transportation efficiency in one of the world's most dynamic cities.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION AND EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS FORGE PARTNERSHIP FOR AUTONOMOUS DRONE INITIATIVES

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) is set to enhance its capabilities in the defense sector following a strategic partnership with EVERYWHERE Communications. The collaboration aims to advance next-generation autonomous drone technology under the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) initiative, focusing on enabling operations in disconnected and challenging environments. The announcement was made on the 4th of May 2026.Modern drone systems often rely on continuous connectivity for effective operation, which can be severely limited in environments where networks are degraded or unavailable. This limitation affects beyond-line-of-sight operations and hampers the timely delivery of critical intelligence. By leveraging Iridium Satellite technology, EVERYWHERE Communications is introducing a resilient data transport layer that allows drones to operate autonomously while transmitting essential sensor data back to operators and command systems.The innovative technology being developed enables several key capabilities, including beyond-line-of-sight operations that reduce reliance on continuous pilot control, reliable data exfiltration to ensure sensor data reaches decision-makers even in disrupted environments, and autonomous mission execution. Furthermore, the platform supports scalable coordination, allowing numerous drones to operate simultaneously while maintaining efficient command and control through low-bandwidth communication channels.Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, emphasized the importance of this technology, stating, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Additionally, Parsons is contributing its TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) solution, which provides scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability.This partnership marks a pivotal development in the push for resilient and intelligent unmanned systems for tactical operations. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, stated, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." As both companies move forward, they aim to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION EXPANDS ROLE IN NEW YORK'S INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENTS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected to manage the construction of the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel in New York City. This announcement comes on the heels of a significant contract awarded on the 14th of April 2026, which highlights the company's commitment to innovative infrastructure solutions. As part of this project, Parsons Corporation will oversee the development of a large-diameter storage tunnel and a dewatering pump station aimed at protecting local waterways from untreated discharges.The New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) has entrusted Parsons Corporation with this critical task, recognizing the firm's extensive experience in managing over 250 tunnel and ocean outfall projects worldwide. This infrastructure initiative is not only intended to modernize New York City's physical assets but also to enhance public health and improve water quality throughout the region. By leveraging its technology-driven approach, Parsons Corporation aims to deliver effective solutions that are essential for urban sustainability.In addition to its work in New York, Parsons Corporation has further solidified its position in the defense sector. A joint venture with GSI Americas has secured a $25 million contract with the United States Coast Guard (USCG). This agreement focuses on providing program management services that are vital to the USCG’s Execution Plan, which includes significant upgrades to shore infrastructure and the implementation of transformational technologies.The partnership with GSI Americas underscores Parsons Corporation's strategic intent to support federal agencies in securing and modernizing critical digital and physical assets. This multifaceted approach not only enhances the operational capabilities of the USCG but also aligns with the company's broader objectives in the defense and cybersecurity markets.Overall, Parsons Corporation's recent contracts in both infrastructure and defense sectors demonstrate its versatility and commitment to delivering integrated solutions that meet the evolving needs of urban environments and national security. As the firm continues to expand its portfolio, investors may find interest in its potential for future growth and innovation in these key areas.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJ1-CKY3-RSDP-X1YK-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE PROJECT IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation is making its entry into the Oklahoma market by securing a contract for the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement project. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry Group in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver this significant infrastructure upgrade. This partnership marks the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the Oklahoma DOT, highlighting the company's ambition to expand its regional presence.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, signifies a major investment in modernizing a critical transportation corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has been operational since 1945 and serves as an essential link for local communities, businesses, and visitors traveling to the Lake Texoma area. With the new bridge, Zachry Construction aims to enhance mobility, safety, and economic activity in southeastern Oklahoma.Zachry and Traylor Bros. will construct a new four-lane bridge that includes modern features such as bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also entails the demolition of the existing bridge, with traffic being maintained on it during the construction phase to minimize disruption to the traveling public. Echoing the significance of this venture, Travis Mross, president of Zachry Construction, stated, 'This project is an important milestone for Zachry, as it strengthens a vital connection for the communities around Lake Texoma.'The project is designed to improve safety, reliability, and resilience for the future. It aims to address the increasing traffic demands in the area while ensuring that local commerce and tourism continue to flourish. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has set a completion target of 1st October 2029 for the Roosevelt Bridge replacement project, which is expected to elevate the standard of infrastructure in the region.Zachry Construction Corporation, a third-generation family business with over a century of experience, will leverage its extensive expertise in heavy and highway construction for this initiative. The joint venture with Traylor Bros., known for its proficiency in complex bridge projects, promises to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and maintains mobility throughout construction. Together, they are poised to provide lasting value to the state and its communities.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORP. ELEVATES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AMID STRATEGIC GROWTH

Zachry Construction Corp. has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, marking a significant transition in leadership for the San Antonio-based company. Mross, who began his career with the family-owned business as an intern in 2000, officially took on the presidential role on 29th April 2026.Over the years, Mross has steadily advanced through the ranks, culminating in his previous position as executive vice president for the past six years. In this role, he played a crucial part in shaping Zachry Construction's strategic direction and enhancing operational efficiency. He succeeds David Zachry, who will now concentrate solely on his responsibilities as CEO."Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," David Zachry stated. He emphasized Mross's deep understanding of the business and his contributions to instilling discipline and accountability, which are essential for the company's continued success. Under Mross's leadership, Zachry Construction aims to maintain its momentum and explore new opportunities.Zachry Construction Corp. is recognized for its extensive work in heavy civil, roadway, and infrastructure projects, with over 6,000 completed initiatives in both public and private sectors. The company is currently expanding its operations in the water and wastewater infrastructure domain, considered one of the fastest-growing segments in the construction industry. Recent projects include water storage and dams for various authorities, and the acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. has further strengthened its capabilities in this area.As president, Mross will oversee operations across multiple sectors, including water, transportation, underground utilities, and civil sitework. His priorities will focus on strategic growth, operational excellence, and nurturing a robust corporate culture while maintaining a gritty mindset. Mross stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW LEASE NEAR LA VILLITA

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.
