"It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES SPOT ON FORTUNE 100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been recognized as one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2026. This acknowledgment comes as a testament to PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace environment, reflecting the company's dedication to employee satisfaction and engagement. The official announcement was made on 2nd April 2026.This marks the 17th time PCL Construction has earned a place on this prestigious list, achieving a notable rank of 91 this year. The recognition highlights PCL's sustained efforts in creating a supportive and empowering work environment, where employees feel valued and motivated to contribute to the company's success.Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of PCL's U.S. operations, emphasized that the company's employee-owned structure plays a critical role in shaping its culture. He noted, 'Our people are directly invested in shaping a culture where they feel trusted, supported and excited about the future.' This approach not only enhances employee morale but also drives the overall performance of PCL Construction.PCL's recognition comes on the heels of a successful year, during which the company also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 Contractors list for 2025. This accolade underscores PCL Construction's leadership in executing complex projects across North America while maintaining its focus on employee well-being and satisfaction.The Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience. + PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. + MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. + AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-K913-SCG7-44M5-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE PROJECT IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation is making its entry into the Oklahoma market by securing a contract for the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement project. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry Group in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver this significant infrastructure upgrade. This partnership marks the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the Oklahoma DOT, highlighting the company's ambition to expand its regional presence.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, signifies a major investment in modernizing a critical transportation corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has been operational since 1945 and serves as an essential link for local communities, businesses, and visitors traveling to the Lake Texoma area. With the new bridge, Zachry Construction aims to enhance mobility, safety, and economic activity in southeastern Oklahoma.Zachry and Traylor Bros. will construct a new four-lane bridge that includes modern features such as bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also entails the demolition of the existing bridge, with traffic being maintained on it during the construction phase to minimize disruption to the traveling public. Echoing the significance of this venture, Travis Mross, president of Zachry Construction, stated, 'This project is an important milestone for Zachry, as it strengthens a vital connection for the communities around Lake Texoma.'The project is designed to improve safety, reliability, and resilience for the future. It aims to address the increasing traffic demands in the area while ensuring that local commerce and tourism continue to flourish. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has set a completion target of 1st October 2029 for the Roosevelt Bridge replacement project, which is expected to elevate the standard of infrastructure in the region.Zachry Construction Corporation, a third-generation family business with over a century of experience, will leverage its extensive expertise in heavy and highway construction for this initiative. The joint venture with Traylor Bros., known for its proficiency in complex bridge projects, promises to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and maintains mobility throughout construction. Together, they are poised to provide lasting value to the state and its communities.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORP. ELEVATES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AMID STRATEGIC GROWTH

Zachry Construction Corp. has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, marking a significant transition in leadership for the San Antonio-based company. Mross, who began his career with the family-owned business as an intern in 2000, officially took on the presidential role on 29th April 2026.Over the years, Mross has steadily advanced through the ranks, culminating in his previous position as executive vice president for the past six years. In this role, he played a crucial part in shaping Zachry Construction's strategic direction and enhancing operational efficiency. He succeeds David Zachry, who will now concentrate solely on his responsibilities as CEO."Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," David Zachry stated. He emphasized Mross's deep understanding of the business and his contributions to instilling discipline and accountability, which are essential for the company's continued success. Under Mross's leadership, Zachry Construction aims to maintain its momentum and explore new opportunities.Zachry Construction Corp. is recognized for its extensive work in heavy civil, roadway, and infrastructure projects, with over 6,000 completed initiatives in both public and private sectors. The company is currently expanding its operations in the water and wastewater infrastructure domain, considered one of the fastest-growing segments in the construction industry. Recent projects include water storage and dams for various authorities, and the acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. has further strengthened its capabilities in this area.As president, Mross will oversee operations across multiple sectors, including water, transportation, underground utilities, and civil sitework. His priorities will focus on strategic growth, operational excellence, and nurturing a robust corporate culture while maintaining a gritty mindset. Mross stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW LEASE NEAR LA VILLITA

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO ENHANCE PIPEFITTING SKILLS IN LAKE CHARLES

Bechtel, a leading global engineering and construction firm, has announced a partnership with SOWELA Technical Community College to introduce a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA’s Main Campus in Lake Charles. This initiative aims to equip local students with the essential skills needed for a career in pipefitting, a critical trade in various industries including manufacturing and power generation. The program is set to commence on 26th May 2026 and will run until 13th November 2026.The training program is designed to provide students with a comprehensive introduction to the pipefitting trade, covering core job responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used in the field. Participants will engage in hands-on learning, focusing on the installation and repair of high- and low-pressure pipe systems, which are vital to the functioning of manufacturing facilities, power generation plants, and building heating and cooling systems.Classes will be held Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., allowing participants to gain a thorough understanding of the pipefitting profession over the course of the program. Upon successful completion, students will earn an OSHA 10-hour safety credential and will be well-prepared to pursue the National Center for Construction Education and Research (NCCER) Pipefitting certifications, enhancing their employability in a competitive job market.To qualify for employment consideration, successful program completers must pass a background check and drug screening, ensuring that they meet industry standards. Interested individuals can find more information or register for the program by visiting www.sowela.edu/pipefitting. For further inquiries, SOWELA’s Office of Workforce Solutions can be contacted at (337) 421-6560 or via email at workforce@sowela.edu.This collaboration between Bechtel and SOWELA highlights the commitment of both organizations to workforce development in the Lake Charles area, addressing the growing demand for skilled labor in the construction and manufacturing sectors. By providing accessible training opportunities, the program aims to empower local residents and contribute to the region 's economic growth.

+ BECHTEL-ENKA CONSORTIUM UNDER PRESSURE TO MEET HIGHWAY DEADLINES IN SERBIA

Serbian President Aleksandar Vu i announced today that he met with representatives of the Bechtel-Enka company, discussing the ongoing construction of a highway that aims to connect multiple cities across Serbia. During this meeting, held on the 21st of April 2026, Vu i emphasized the importance of adhering to the project timelines, particularly for the section stretching from a ak to Kraljevo.Vu i stated, "I am particularly adamant about respecting the deadlines in the a ak-Kraljevo section, which holds significant importance for all of western and southwestern Serbia." This section is part of a larger initiative, the Moravian Corridor, that is seen as one of the most complex infrastructural projects in Serbia's recent history. The government views the timely completion of this highway as crucial for regional development and connectivity.The Bechtel-Enka consortium has faced scrutiny in the past regarding project timelines and budget overruns in various international projects. As Serbia continues to invest heavily in infrastructure development, the pressure is mounting on Bechtel-Enka to deliver as promised. Vu i 's insistence on meeting deadlines reflects the government's broader strategy to enhance economic growth through improved transportation networks.This highway project not only aims to facilitate domestic travel and commerce but also seeks to attract foreign investment by improving Serbia's infrastructure. The government believes that a well-developed transport network will contribute positively to the overall economic landscape of the country, making timely project completion all the more critical.As the Bechtel-Enka consortium moves forward with the construction, stakeholders will be closely monitoring progress to ensure that the timelines set by Vu i are met. The outcome of this project could have significant implications for both the consortium and the Serbian economy as a whole.

+ BECHTEL'S CRAIG ALBERT FUELS NEW CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM AT UNC CHARLOTTE

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have made a significant leadership gift to the university, aimed at launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation’s fastest-growing markets. This initiative will be housed within The Albert School of Construction in the William States Lee College of Engineering. The school will emphasize the full capital project lifecycle, preparing students to lead the planning, engineering, and delivery of complex infrastructure projects. This announcement comes on the 7th of April, 2026.Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber highlighted the importance of this investment, stating, "This investment allows us to align education more directly with the needs of our region and the nation." As Charlotte and North Carolina continue to grow, the complexity of infrastructure demands increases, necessitating graduates who are equipped to contribute effectively. Craig Albert, the president and chief operating officer of Bechtel Group, emphasized the pressing need for a skilled workforce to support essential industries, noting a real shortage of construction capacity.The Albert School of Construction aims to build upon the university’s 50 years of construction education by integrating disciplines across engineering, business, and data science into a cohesive academic framework. Rob Keynton, dean of the William States Lee College of Engineering, explained that the program’s unique approach encompasses the entire capital project lifecycle. This comprehensive perspective positions UNC Charlotte as a leader in the evolution of construction education, emphasizing industry partnerships and research strengths.Leading the school will be Executive Director Lingguang Song, who has a background as a professor and department chair in engineering technology and construction management. By offering a curriculum that aligns closely with industry needs, the program aspires to ensure graduates can adapt quickly to professional demands. Craig Albert stated, "My hope is that companies will see graduates from this program climbing the learning curve more quickly. They’ll be equipped for exactly what the industry needs."The Alberts’ leadership gift marks the initial phase of a broader initiative to expand the school’s facilities, programs, and industry collaborations. Future plans include the development of hands-on learning environments, an integrated curriculum with industry input, and endowed faculty positions in critical construction areas. The Alberts are also co-chairs of the For the Love of Charlotte campaign, initiated to raise a record $500 million for the university. Craig Albert expressed a desire for other successful alumni to follow their lead in supporting UNC Charlotte’s growth and impact.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INNSBROOK OFFICE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. The expansion is supported by local incentives from Henrico County, which aims to attract and retain businesses in the area. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The new office space will allow DPR Construction to bring together teams and resources, fostering collaboration and innovation within the company. This growth is expected to create additional job opportunities in the region, further contributing to Henrico's economic development goals. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. This aligns with the company's mission to lead in sustainable construction practices while also providing a conducive work environment for its employees. The expansion is anticipated to be completed within the next year, further solidifying DPR Construction's commitment to the region.In addition to the expansion, DPR Construction has been actively involved in various community initiatives, underscoring its dedication to corporate social responsibility. As the company prepares for this new chapter, stakeholders are keenly watching how this expansion will impact both the firm and the local economy.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION EXPANDS ROLE IN NEW YORK'S INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENTS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected to manage the construction of the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel in New York City. This announcement comes on the heels of a significant contract awarded on the 14th of April 2026, which highlights the company's commitment to innovative infrastructure solutions. As part of this project, Parsons Corporation will oversee the development of a large-diameter storage tunnel and a dewatering pump station aimed at protecting local waterways from untreated discharges.The New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) has entrusted Parsons Corporation with this critical task, recognizing the firm's extensive experience in managing over 250 tunnel and ocean outfall projects worldwide. This infrastructure initiative is not only intended to modernize New York City's physical assets but also to enhance public health and improve water quality throughout the region. By leveraging its technology-driven approach, Parsons Corporation aims to deliver effective solutions that are essential for urban sustainability.In addition to its work in New York, Parsons Corporation has further solidified its position in the defense sector. A joint venture with GSI Americas has secured a $25 million contract with the United States Coast Guard (USCG). This agreement focuses on providing program management services that are vital to the USCG’s Execution Plan, which includes significant upgrades to shore infrastructure and the implementation of transformational technologies.The partnership with GSI Americas underscores Parsons Corporation's strategic intent to support federal agencies in securing and modernizing critical digital and physical assets. This multifaceted approach not only enhances the operational capabilities of the USCG but also aligns with the company's broader objectives in the defense and cybersecurity markets.Overall, Parsons Corporation's recent contracts in both infrastructure and defense sectors demonstrate its versatility and commitment to delivering integrated solutions that meet the evolving needs of urban environments and national security. As the firm continues to expand its portfolio, investors may find interest in its potential for future growth and innovation in these key areas.

+ PARSONS TEAMS WITH GSIA FOR $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD CONTRACT

CHANTILLY, Va. — Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) has been awarded a contract valued at $25 million by the United States Coast Guard (USCG) for program management services. This contract, granted on 15th April 2026, includes a one-year base period of performance with four additional one-year option periods.The scope of this contract entails providing program management services that will facilitate the USCG’s ambitious Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, which represents the largest capital investment in the history of the Coast Guard. This comprehensive initiative aims to enhance USCG capabilities through significant upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure, supported by a total budget of $24.5 billion allocated for modernizing platforms and systems, improving training facilities, and boosting mission-critical operational capacities.Martin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, expressed enthusiasm for the partnership, stating, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this historic modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." As a leader in program management, Parsons brings a wealth of expertise and integrated services essential for the successful execution of this significant plan.GSIA, known for its innovative and sustainable energy solutions, will also play a pivotal role in this joint venture. Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, commented, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness, modernizes critical infrastructure, and strengthens the nation’s maritime security posture."With a strong track record in managing complex projects, Parsons is recognized as the top program management services firm by Engineering News-Record. The company provides comprehensive support throughout the project lifecycle, including master planning, design, construction management, and asset management. This partnership with GSIA further underscores Parsons' commitment to delivering impactful solutions in national security and infrastructure.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION WINS $409M ROOSEVELT BRIDGE DESIGN PROJECT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected as the lead designer for the Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project, a significant infrastructure initiative valued at $409 million. The announcement was made on 14th April 2026, detailing the project’s aim to replace an aging crossing over Lake Texoma in Bryan and Marshall Counties, Oklahoma.The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has awarded the construction contract to the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture, while Parsons will handle the design aspects of the new bridge. This structure will span approximately two miles over one of the largest reservoirs in the United States, which is managed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. The design will include 63 spans, with substructure elements extending about 150 feet below the lake surface.To facilitate ongoing traffic flow during construction, the project will be executed in two phases. The original Roosevelt Bridge, constructed in 1945, serves as a vital connector for local residents, freight, and travelers. The replacement initiative has secured Oklahoma’s largest federal grant amounting to $124 million in 2024, complemented by state funding.Despite the recent contract win, Parsons’ stock has experienced a decline of 35% over the past six months, currently trading at $56.01, with a market capitalization of approximately $6 billion. Analysts have indicated that the stock may be undervalued relative to its fair value, positioning it as a potential opportunity for investors. The company’s president of Infrastructure North America, Mark Fialkowski, emphasized Parsons’ legacy in bridge design, stating that this project exemplifies their commitment to delivering complex infrastructure solutions worldwide.In addition to the Roosevelt Bridge project, Parsons has been active in various significant initiatives, including a 10-year contract with the Utah Department of Transportation for a Vehicle-to-Everything Maintenance and Management System. Furthermore, Parsons is leading the design for a $389 million project on Interstate 64 in Virginia and has been appointed as the project management consultant for the Al Ittihad Sports Village in Saudi Arabia. Despite these advancements, Baird recently downgraded Parsons’ stock rating to Neutral, citing concerns over potential revenue declines in the Middle East, illustrating the complex landscape the company navigates in its growth trajectory.

May 2nd, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Tetra Tech (NASDAQ:TTEK) reported fiscal 2026 second-quarter results that management said reflected rising demand for high-end consulting and design services across water, environmental, and sustainable infrastructure markets, while also delivering margin expansion and record cash flow in the first half of the year.

Roger Argus, who is leading his first quarterly earnings call as chief executive officer, said the companys strategy is unchanged and remains focused on high-end solutions that address the complex challenges where our clients need us most. Argus also recognized former CEO Dan Batracks leadership and said he is continuing as executive chairman.

Second-quarter performance and segment results

Argus said net revenue increased 8% year-over-year in the second quarter, while EBITDA totaled $146 million and represented a 90-basis-point margin expansion from the prior year. He called it an all-time record for a second quarter.

Earnings per share were $0.36, which included $0.02 tied to the completion of the divestiture of Norwegian operations. Adjusted EPS was $0.34, which Argus said exceeded the high end of guidance and was the companys highest for any second quarter.

By segment, Argus said the Government Services Group (GSG) grew 5% year-over-year and generated a margin of 16.3%, up 220 basis points from the prior year, driven by demand across water, environment, defense, and resilient infrastructure. The Commercial International Group (CIG) posted 10% year-over-year revenue growth and a 12.2% margin, with Argus citing a diversified client mix in water, environmental, power, and energy markets.

During Q&A, management addressed the gap between the segment margins. Argus said CIG typically has seasonal weakness in the second quarter due to winter fieldwork constraints in parts of the Northern Hemisphere and holiday patterns in Australia, but he and CFO Steven Burdick said they expect CIG margins to improve through the balance of the year and that the two segments margins could move closer together.

Client market trends: federal strength, commercial mixed, international up

Argus broke down net revenue performance by customer type:

U.S. federal work rose 11% year-over-year and represented 20% of the business, supported by U.S. Army Corps of Engineers work in flood protection and inland navigation, defense facility modernization, and planning and permitting programs for defense clients.

U.S. state and local work increased 9% and represented 14% of the business, driven by municipal water projects, particularly in Florida, Texas, California, and Virginia.

U.S. commercial represented 19% of the business and declined 2% year-over-year. Argus said growth in energy and transmission-related services was offset by reduced renewable energy services as large offshore wind programs from the prior year wind down.

International work increased 12% year-over-year, driven by water services in the U.K., Ireland, and the Netherlands, infrastructure services in Canada, and digital automation revenues in Australia.

On international demand, Argus told analysts that the outlook reflects both geopolitical and local drivers. He highlighted AMP8 funding in the U.K., which he said is double the funding from AMP7, as well as activity in Canada tied to infrastructure funding and a recovery in Australia supported by mining, defense shore facilities, and potential opportunities connected to infrastructure spending ahead of the 2032 Brisbane Olympics.

Backlog rises sequentially as budget clarity supports orders

Argus said backlog increased 8% sequentially to $4.28 billion, which he described as demonstrating the resilience of the companys Leading with Science approach. He emphasized Tetra Techs conservative backlog methodology, stating the company includes only work that is contracted, funded, and authorized.

Key backlog wins cited by Argus included:

More than $650 million in added contract capacity from U.S. defense clients for water and resilient infrastructure services

A GBP 18 million single-award contract in Northern Ireland for water and wastewater treatment services

A framework contract in the Netherlands to expand capacity for flood protection and infrastructure modernization

A master service agreement at the Port of Los Angeles

An expansion of work with United Utilities in the U.K. tied to Tetra Techs WaterNet software for leakage detection and water delivery modernization

In response to questions about backlog momentum, Argus said the resolution of much of the U.S. federal budget in early Q2 drove an uptick in new federal task orders, including work from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command, and the Air Force Civil Engineer Center. He said management views Q2 as an inflection point for backlog and expects continued growth through the rest of the fiscal year.

Burdick added that with USAID-related backlog reductions (which he said tended to be longer-duration), a greater portion of the remaining backlog is now shorter-term, contributing to more book and burn activity than in prior years.

Cash flow, capital allocation, and contract mix

Burdick said that while reported revenue was down year-over-year due to lower USA customer revenue and the absence of one-time disaster work, operating income increased and first-half adjusted EBITDA on net revenue expanded 110 basis points to 14%. He said this supports the companys long-term goal of improving EBITDA margins by 50 basis points annually.

For the first half, Burdick reported record operating cash flow of $238 million. He also said days sales outstanding improved to 58 days, a nine-day improvement from the prior year period. Burdick reported net debt of about $657 million, with net leverage at 1.0x EBITDA, down from 1.36x a year earlier. He also said return on capital employed is now over 20%.

On capital allocation, Burdick said the companys balance sheet is probably the strongest in its history and cited trailing 12-month operating cash flow of $688 million. He said Tetra Tech has closed acquisitions of defense-focused technical leaders, including Halvik in the U.S. and Providence in Australia.

Burdick also announced the board approved an 11% year-over-year increase in the quarterly cash dividend to be paid in the third quarter, marking the 44th consecutive quarterly dividend with annual double-digit increases. The company repurchased $100 million of stock in the first half of fiscal 2026 and has $498 million remaining under its authorization.

Several analysts asked about working capital and contract mix. Burdick said the company believes it can reduce DSO closer to 50 days over the next two years and noted that fixed-price contracts tend to carry both higher margins and lower working capital requirements. He said fixed-price work rose from about 37% of net revenue in 2023 to about 48% year-to-date, and in GSG from about 29% last year to about 42% this year.

Outlook: raised guidance and higher second-half growth expectations

Argus said management is increasing its forecasted growth rates for the second half for both U.S. federal and U.S. commercial clients to 8% to 12%, noting those sectors represent about 40% of revenue. He said federal growth is expected as funding is deployed for domestic civil works and defense facility modernization globally. Commercial growth is expected to be supported by mining water management, rare earth mine development, and front-end planning and permitting for power generation and transmission.

International work is expected to grow 5% to 10% with strength in U.K., Ireland, and Netherlands water and marine defense infrastructure spending in the U.K. and Australia. Argus said state and local work is expected to be about 15% of the business with growth of 5% to 10%.

Argus addressed the lower state and local growth range versus prior expectations, saying municipal clients are acting cautiously around potential reductions in supplemental federal grant funding and are looking to alternative funding methodssuch as rate increases, bond issuance, and funding restructuringto keep projects moving. Burdick added that the growth is occurring on a higher base after the state and local share of net revenue rose from about 11% in 2024 to about 14% in fiscal 2025.

For guidance, Argus provided the following ranges:

Q3 net revenue: $1.05 billion to $1.1 billion

Q3 adjusted EPS: $0.38 to $0.41

FY 2026 net revenue: $4.25 billion to $4.4 billion

FY 2026 adjusted EPS: $1.50 to $1.58

Argus said the midpoint of the full-year net revenue outlook implies 9% year-over-year growth, along with 70 basis points of margin expansion at the midpoint. He also cited several guidance assumptions, including intangible amortization of $33 million, depreciation of $24 million, interest expense of $33 million, and an effective tax rate of 27.5%. He said the guidance does not include contributions from future acquisitions.

During Q&A, Burdick said foreign exchange impact in the quarter was fairly minimal, and he confirmed there was effectively no one-time disaster revenue in Q2 compared with the prior years hurricanes in Florida and fires in Southern California. He also said the company recorded about $61 million of USAID work in the quarter, primarily related to increased activity in Ukraine, and that guidance assumes about $20 million per quarter in Q3 and Q4.

Argus concluded by saying the company is entering the second half with strong backlog and that demand for its differentiated services continues to drive growth, leading management to raise full-year fiscal 2026 guidance.

About Tetra Tech (NASDAQ:TTEK)

Tetra Tech, Inc is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services with a focus on water, environment, infrastructure, resource management and energy sectors. Headquartered in Pasadena, California, the company delivers end-to-end solutions that encompass planning, design, engineering, program management and construction management. Tetra Tech's multidisciplinary teams integrate science, technology and advisory services to address complex challenges in areas such as water resources, environmental remediation, sustainable infrastructure and renewable energy.

The company's core offerings include environmental assessments and cleanup, water treatment and reuse, coastal and marine engineering, climate resilience planning, and engineering design for transportation and built environments.

Link to the original story.
